codeReview.summary: The feature substantially implements cursor replay, deterministic startup, and stable event IDs, but it has a blocking resume gap: a reconnect cursor based on the emitted connected event can skip replay events that were not yet delivered if the client disconnects during catch-up.

issues.description: `connectedEventId` is registered as a cursor position before replay (`registerCursorPosition(connectedEventId)`), and that cursor points to the current end of the event log (`src/relay/message-store.ts:516`). Because replay is sent after `connected`, a client that disconnects after receiving `connected` but before receiving replayed missed events will reconnect with `Last-Event-ID=connectedEventId` and `getEventsSince` will return only events after the old log tail, dropping those missed logical transitions. This violates VAL-STREAM-003's no-missing-events reconnect guarantee under partial-replay disconnects.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The skill requires manual CLI/API/SSE sanity checks (or explicit rationale for skipping) for user-facing stream changes, but the handoff only lists automated commands while still marking `followedProcedure=true`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/mors-global-worker/SKILL.md:31 requires manual CLI/API/SSE checks; handoff file /Users/justen/.factory/missions/54bd84c5-2680-4f00-890c-9c832860b3d4/handoffs/2026-02-28T15-22-32-916Z__sse-resume-cursor-determinism-and-dedupe__418dcf23-fb85-4539-ac7a-037c2b48f9fe.json includes `verification.commandsRun` only and no manual-check section, while `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` is true.

summary: Review failed due to a blocking SSE resume correctness issue: connected-event cursor registration occurs before replay and can cause missed events when reconnect happens during catch-up. Shared-state note: skill procedure/manual-check reporting appears inconsistent with the recorded handoff evidence.
